title: Collision Events, Single Resolution, Fragments, Damage, and Lives · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs collision events, single resolution, fragments, damage, and lives from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for ordered single-resolution collision response after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Separate collision facts from response decisions so detection stays pure and effects occur in an owned phase.
Assign pair identity and terminal ownership so score, deletion, sound, fragments, and telemetry happen once.
Spawn child sizes, positions, velocities, immunity, and counts after parent resolution under entity budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Response Order
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 3229 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve each collision once with explicit destruction, fragments, lives, invulnerability, feedback, and event ordering; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Specify response event ordering and test simultaneous projectile-rock, ship-rock, wave-clear, and life-loss cases.

Inspect the Response Order trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Response Order.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Response Order, keep Response Order connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Response Order, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Specify response event ordering and test simultaneous projectile-rock, ship-rock, wave-clear, and life-loss cases.
